title: Licania splendens
intro: Licania splendens, also known as the yellow sapote or sapote de monte, is a plant species in the family Chrysobalanaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Amazon region, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and protecting against infections. The bioactive constituents of Licania splendens include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, colds, and respiratory issues.
